description_long: This object is a rectangular wooden planter box filled with brown soil and containing three distinct succulent plants. The largest plant is a pale green prickly pear cactus (Opuntia) with three rounded pads, each dotted with small, light-colored areoles. In front of the cactus are two smaller succulents: one with broad, blue-green rosette leaves resembling an Echeveria, and another with pointed, yellow-green leaves similar to an Agave or Aloe. The planter box is constructed from dark, weathered wood planks, giving it a rustic appearance. The soil surface is even and slightly textured, with no visible mulch or decorative stones. There are no visible drainage holes or additional decorative elements on the planter itself.
description: A rustic wooden planter box holds a prickly pear cactus and two types of succulents in brown soil. The arrangement features a mix of pale green, blue-green, and yellow-green foliage.
description_view_0: The front view shows all three plants clearly, with the prickly pear cactus in the back and the two succulents in the foreground, along with the wood grain of the planter.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the thin profile of the cactus and the arrangement of the two succulents at the front edge of the planter.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the cactus pads prominently, with the succulents partially visible and the wood texture of the back panel.
description_view_3: The opposite side view shows the cactus in profile and the succulents from a different angle, emphasizing the depth of the planter box.
